appTagline: Create, edit and play your card games
revertToTheLastSavedVersion: Revert to the last saved version
AtEachChangeAutoSaveWillBeDone: At each change, an auto-save will be done. You can revert to the last saved version at any time.
welcomeSubtitle: Log in or create an account to continue
ForgotPasswordFormSubtitle: You will receive a recovery code in your mailbox once the form is submitted.
doYouRealyWantToRestore: Do you really want to restore the saved version? Unsaved data will be lost
legalNotice: By logging in, you accept our
password_recovery_info: You will receive a recovery code in your inbox once the form is submitted.
errorLength: You must enter at least {{length}} characters
searchGame: Find game with name, description or type...
selectGameSubtitle: Choose the game you want to configure and manage. You will be able to modify its settings, create cards, and start games.
wantToSwitchGameToVisible: Would you like to make this game publicly visible on the CARD Games interface?
publicVisibilityHelperText: The game will appear in the list of available games for all users. Otherwise, the game will not be listed in the public interface. It will only be accessible to users who possess and enter a unique access code.
commentAreCollectOnGame: Comments are collected directly from the gaming platform
playerConfiguration_hint: Configure the number and conditions of the players
allowSpectators_description: Allow users to join the game as spectators. When joining a game via its ID, users will be able to choose their status (Player or Spectator).
soloMode_description: Enable this mode to play alone. This prevents users from joining as visitors or spectators for this instance.
playerParticipation_description: Check this option to allow other players to join the game. If Solo Mode is selected: Players joining the game will be automatically designated as Spectators. Otherwise, players joining the game will become Active Participants.
unsavedChangesWarning: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave this page?
roleManagementDescription: Definition and Allocation: Roles allow you to assign customized functions and powers to each player in the game. Roles with Specific Powers: Used in games where certain participants possess unique abilities (Example: The different roles in Werewolf). Structural Game Roles: Used to define responsibilities essential to the game mechanics (Example: The Dealer in Blackjack).
tourManagementDescription: Definition of Turns: A turn represents a unit of time or action in the game. You can configure the actions that occur each turn, such as dealing cards, changing the active player, or triggering specific events. Example of a Turn: In a traditional card game, a turn may consist of dealing cards to each player, followed by an action phase where players can play their cards or perform specific actions.
rewardsDefinitionDescription: Earnings Definition: This section allows you to set up the rewards players will receive following a specific action, an in-game event, or the resolution of a round. Currency Example: In Poker, chips are distributed as winnings after each successful round.
assetLibraryDescription: Explore and download resources for your cards
eventDescription: All events that are not global and are triggered by a demon or an action. This excludes events with specific values. It only includes events with fixed values that repeat.
demonDescription: A demon monitors the game and triggers based on a specific condition. It can act at any time during the game. For example, it could remove points from a player when they hold a specific card or distribute all gains at the start of the game.
eventsAndTriggersManagementDescription: This section allows you to manage everything related to game actions, whether it's for distributing cards, changing variables, or incrementing turns; it is the core of the game's mechanics.
eventConfigurationDescription: Define the event name and type
triggerConditionPlaceholder: Describe when this event should trigger...
eventActiveDescription: The event can trigger during the game
triggeredActionsDescription: Define what happens when the event is triggered
activationConditionDescription: Before activating an event, we will verify if this condition can be fulfilled
removeAllAtachedEventsForTour: Remove all attached events for tour
allPlayersHasPlayed/endOfTour: Have all players played (end of turn) ?
victory-trigger-description: This event automatically triggers victory for the specified people
features-description: You can compare elements, add conditions, and perform calculations
condition-in-boucle-description: Condition to check before performing the event's actions
attachedEventForTour: Associate an item with the player
createGameInstruction: Go to "Game Edition" and click on "New game". You can choose a pre-configured template or start from scratch.
createCardsInstruction: Use "Card Creation" to design your cards with custom images, text, and attributes.
configureRulesInstruction: In "Advanced Settings", define the game flow, turns, events, and victory conditions.
testYourGameInstruction: Use "Multiplayer" mode to test your game alone or invite other players.
useTemplatesTip: Pre-configured templates save you time and provide examples of best practices.
organizeColorsTip: In "Color Management", create a consistent palette for your card types and elements.
defineVariablesEarlyTip: Set up your global variables from the start in "Global Variables" to make further development easier.
testRegularlyTip: Don't wait until you're finished to test your game. Regular testing helps you catch problems early.
saveYourAssetsTip: Use the "Asset Library" to centralize your images and reuse them easily.
howToChangeGameAnswer: Click the "Change game" button in the header at the top of the screen to return to the game selection.
canIImportImages: Can I import my own images?
canIImportImagesAnswer: Yes, go to the "Asset Library" to upload your custom images.
howToInvitePlayersAnswer: In the "Multiplayer" section, create a game and share the game code with your friends.
areChangesSavedAutomaticallyAnswer: Yes, if auto-save is enabled in the settings. You can also save manually using the "Save" button.
youDeleteINtegralGameWithoutSave: By clicking 'Delete' you delete the entire game. It will no longer be visible in the game list. Data will not be recoverable.
doYouRealyWantToDeleteGame: Do you really want to delete the game?
doYouRealyWantToDelete: Do you really want to delete this item?
FailedToDeleteGame: An error occurred while deleting the game
FailedToCreateGame: An error occurred while creating the game
FailedToRetrieveGame: An error occurred while retrieving the game
FailedToUpdateGame: An error occurred while updating the game
doYouRealyWantToDeleteEvent: Do you really want to delete this event?
youDeleteEventWithoutSave: By clicking 'Delete' you delete the event. It will no longer be visible in the event list. Data will not be recoverable.
globalManagementForTours: General settings for the round system
actionForEachTour: Manage actions that occur each round
maxNumberOfToursInGame: Maximum number of rounds in a game
positionOfPlayerWhoHasToPlay: Position of the player who must play first
definedPlayerWhoHasToPlayFirst: Specific player who must play first
writeExpressionToCalculateFirstPlayer: Write an expression to calculate the player who must play first (e.g.: player with the most cards in hand)
actionOfPlayerAtTurn: Action of the player each round
manageActionOfPlayerAtTurn: Manage actions that occur each round for the active player
playersCanPlayInSameTime: Players can play at the same time. If this option is disabled, players must wait their turn to play.
youDeleteDemonWithoutSave: By clicking 'Delete' you delete the demon. It will no longer be visible in the demon list. Data will not be recoverable.
doYouRealyWantToDeleteDemon: Do you really want to delete this demon?
atEndOfTime: At the end of the time limit
actionOfPlayerAtTurnDescription: Manage actions that occur each round for the active player. For example, you can make them draw a card, lose points, or trigger a specific event.
actionAppearAtPlayerTurn: Action to perform each round for the active player
ifTrueActionWillAppearAtPlayerTurn: If this option is enabled, the configured action will appear each round for the active player. This means the player can perform this action every time it's their turn. If unchecked, the action will be available at any time during the game, regardless of the round.
name: Name
youDeleteActionWithoutSave: By clicking 'Delete' you delete the action. It will no longer be visible in the action list. Data will not be recoverable.
doYouReallyWantToDeleteAction: Do you really want to delete this action?
actionNameCannotBeEmpty: The action name cannot be empty
pleaseProvideEventsForAction: Please provide events associated with this action
hereIsAllDemonWichCallThisEvent: Here are all the demons that call this event. By clicking on a demon, you can access its configuration and see under what conditions it calls this event.
eventNameCannotBeEmpty: The event name cannot be empty
demonNameCannotBeEmpty: The demon name cannot be empty
eventWithValueNameCannotBeEmpty: The dynamic value event name cannot be empty
demonEventsMustNotBeEmpty: Events associated with a demon cannot be empty
demonConditionMustNotBeEmpty: The demon activation condition cannot be empty
demonConfigurationDescription: Define the name, activation condition, and events associated with this demon
demon-event-description: Events associated with this demon will be triggered when the demon's activation condition is met. For example, you can create a demon that checks if a player has more than 5 cards in hand, and associate with this demon an event that makes them lose points each time this condition is met.
withValueEventDescription: A dynamic value event is a type of event that requires a specific value to be triggered. This value can be determined by player input, a calculation based on the current game state, or a specific condition. For example, you can create a dynamic value event that is triggered when a player's points reach a certain threshold, or when a player enters a specific value during their turn.
calledInThisWithValueEventt: Dynamic value events that execute this event
hereIsAllEventWichCallThisEvent: Here are all the events that call this event. By clicking on an event, you can access its configuration and see under what conditions it calls this event.
hereIsAllWithValueWichCallThisEvent: Here are all the dynamic value events that call this event. By clicking on an event, you can access its configuration and see under what conditions it calls this event.
visibility-display-description: Configure the visibility of elements during the game
display-handDeck-description: Displays the player's hand during the game. If this option is disabled, players will not be able to see the cards in their hand during the game. This can add a dimension of mystery and strategy, as players will have to remember the cards in their hand without being able to consult them visually. However, this can also make the game more difficult, especially for new players, as they will have to remember their cards without being able to see them. This option is particularly suitable for memory games or games where memory plays an important role in the game strategy.
display-count-adversary-handdeck: Number of cards in opponents' hands
display-count-adversary-handdeck-descrip: Displays the number of cards in opponents' hands during the game. If this option is enabled, players will be able to see how many cards their opponents have in hand during the game. This can help players make strategic decisions.
display-statistics-description: Displays game statistics during the game. If this option is enabled, players will be able to see statistics such as the number of cards played, points accumulated, or other relevant data during the game. This can help players track their progress and make strategic decisions based on this information.
display-timer-description: Displays a timer during the game. If this option is enabled, players will see a timer that can be used to limit thinking time or to track the total duration of the game. This can add a dimension of time pressure to the game, encouraging players to make decisions more quickly and maintain a dynamic game pace.
display-chat-description: Displays a chat during the game. If this option is enabled, players will have access to an integrated chat where they can communicate with each other during the game. This can promote social interaction and collaboration between players, allowing them to discuss strategy, form alliances, or simply socialize during the game. However, this can also introduce distractions, so it's important to consider the type of game and player preferences before enabling this option.
display-history-description: Displays a history of actions during the game. If this option is enabled, players will be able to see a detailed history of all actions that have taken place during the game, such as cards played, points won or lost, and triggered events. This can help players track the progress of the game, analyze strategies used, and make informed decisions based on past actions. However, this can also make the game more complex, especially for new players, as they will have to follow a lot of information during the game.
roundsManagementTimerDescription: Configure a timer for game rounds. You can set a time limit for each round.
rounds-timer-activation: Rounds are limited by a timer.
rounds-timer-activation-description: If this option is enabled, game rounds will be limited by a timer. This means that each round will have a limited duration, and when the timer expires, the round ends automatically and the game moves to the next round. This feature can add a dimension of time pressure to the game, encouraging players to make decisions more quickly.
maxNumberOfMance: Maximum number of matches in a game
FailedToUploadImage: An error occurred while uploading the image
onThisPageYouWillFindAllSettingsToConfig: On this page, you will find all the settings to configure the rounds and matches of your game. You can set the maximum number of rounds and matches, the order in which they take place, and other advanced settings to customize the progress of your game.
displayErrorsAndWarningsToggle: If this option is enabled, errors and warnings will be displayed during the game. This can be useful for identifying configuration or gameplay issues, but it can also be distracting for players. It is recommended to enable this option only during the development and testing phase of the game.
FailedToRetrieveUserData: An error occurred while retrieving user data
FailedToModifyUserData: An error occurred while modifying user data
thisElementWillBeVisibleInGame: This element will be visible during the game if this option is enabled. Otherwise, it will not be displayed during the game, but it can still be used in the game's rules and events.
calledInTheseWithValueEvent: Called in these dynamic value events
noWithValueEvent: No dynamic value events called by this event
withValueEventDoesnotExist: The selected dynamic value event does not exist
visualiseYourGameSchema: Visualize your game schema with the flowchart tool. This tool allows you to create a visual representation of your game's mechanics, including the flow of events, player actions, and game states. You can use this visualization to better understand and optimize your game design.
caractereMax75: The name must be less than 75 characters
ifThereIsNoCardConfigurationAllCardWillB: If there is no card configuration, all cards will be in the deck by default.
howToConfigureDistributionOfCard: To set up card distribution, start by navigating to the Events section, select the appropriate category, and create a new entry with the name of your choice. In the Concerned Entity section, select the source deck—typically {deck}, or any other location where your cards are held at the start of the game. If the action should apply to all participants, be sure to select the 'All players in the game' option. For the Targeted Entity, define the cards' destination by using the {playerBoucle#handDeck} variable. Simply enter the number of cards to be dealt in the 'Give an element' field before saving your configuration. Finally, remember to link this event to a specific Demon to ensure it executes automatically at the desired moment during the match.
noEventInGame: There is no event in this game yet. Create your first event with value event to start configuring your game mechanics!
globalValueDescription: Variable used to manage calculations or store game states.
playerGlobalValueDescription: Variable used to manage calculations or store game states for a specific player.
globalValueStaticDescription: Variable used to manage calculations or store game states with a caclulated value.
attachThisActionOnDiscardDeck: Attach this action to the discard pile
attachThisActionOnDiscardDeckDescription: If enabled, this action will not appear in the standard action bar. Instead, the user must click directly on the discard pile to trigger the effect. This allows for specific interactions with discarded cards, adding a layer of strategy to the game's discard management. Setting available event if this option is enable in card management and if no action use this settings.
attachThisActionOnDeck: Attach this action to the deck
attachThisActionOnDeckDescription: If enabled, this action will not appear in the standard action bar. Instead, the user must click directly on the deck (draw pile) to trigger the effect. This is ideal for creating custom mechanics related to drawing cards or inspecting the deck. Setting available event if this option is enable in card management and if no action use this settings.
discardDeckifThisSettingsIsTrueYouCanAcc: If this setting is enabled, you can access multiple settings related to the deck or discard pile in the card management section. This allows you to create specific interactions and mechanics for these elements, such as triggering events when a card is drawn from the deck or discarded into the discard pile.
deckifThisSettingsIsTrueYouCanAccessToMu: If this setting is enabled, you can access multiple settings related to the deck or discard pile in the card management section. This allows you to create specific interactions and mechanics for these elements, such as triggering events when a card is drawn from the deck or discarded into the discard pile.
hereYouCanManageAllSettingsConcernedCard: Here you can manage all settings related to cards, decks, and discard piles. This includes configuring specific interactions, such as triggering events when a card is drawn from the deck or discarded into the discard pile. You can also set up custom mechanics for these elements to create a unique gaming experience.
renderTheLastDiscardedCardDescription: If enabled, the last card sent to the discard pile will be displayed face-up on top. Otherwise, only the card back or the discard pile icon will be visible.
renderTheNextDeckCardDescription: Makes the card on top of the draw pile visible (face-up). This is useful for gameplay mechanics where players can anticipate the next draw.
hereYouCanConfigureWhenWinAppendAndVicto: Here you can configure when a victory occurs and the victory conditions. A victory condition is a specific situation or set of criteria that, when met, results in a player's victory. For example, in a card game, a victory condition could be having a certain number of points, collecting specific cards, or being the last player remaining. By configuring victory conditions, you can define clear goals for players and create engaging gameplay dynamics.
winApplyOnAllPlayers: The victory condition applies to all players
winApplyOnAllPlayersDescription: If enabled, a player's victory immediately ends the game for everyone. If disabled, only the winning players complete the game and become spectators, while the others continue to play.
manyPlayersCanBeWinnerDescription: If this option is enabled, multiple players can meet the victory condition and be declared winners. This is ideal for games where players can achieve the victory condition independently, such as collecting a certain number of points or specific cards. However, if this option is disabled, only the first player to meet the victory condition will be declared the winner, creating a more competitive environment where players race to achieve the victory condition first.
countPointsDescription: If this option, points will be visible on the win screen
valueToBaseCountOfPoints: Value to base the count of points on
valueToBaseCountOfPointsDescription: Select the variable or element that will be used to calculate the points for the victory condition. This could be a player's score, the number of specific cards collected, or any other relevant variable in the game.
conditionToSatisfyDescription: Define the specific condition that must be satisfied for the victory condition to be met. This could involve reaching a certain score, collecting specific cards, or any other criteria relevant to your game's mechanics. For example, you could set a condition that requires a player to have at least 10 points and possess a specific card in their hand to achieve victory.
winConditionLoopDescription: If this option is enabled, the victory condition will be checked continuously throughout the game. This means that as soon as the specified condition is met, the victory will be triggered immediately. This is ideal for games where victory can occur at any time based on players' actions and decisions. However, if this option is disabled, the victory condition will only be checked at specific moments in the game, such as at the end of a round or when a certain event occurs, allowing for more strategic gameplay where players can plan their moves to meet the victory condition at the right moment.
allElementOfBoucleMustSatisyCondition: All elements of the loop must satisfy the condition for the victory to be triggered.
allElementOfBoucleMustSatisyConditionDes: If this option is enabled, all elements of the loop (e.g., all players) must satisfy the victory condition for the victory to be triggered. This means that if you have configured a victory condition that applies to all players, then all players must meet this condition for the game to end and for the players to be declared winners. If this option is disabled, then it is sufficient for only one player to meet the victory condition for the game to end and for that player to be declared the winner.
playerWichWeDisplayPointsDescription: Player whose points are displayed on the victory screen when the 'Count points' option is enabled. This can be the active player, a specific player, or all players in the game, depending on your game's mechanics and how you want to present the victory conditions.
playerWichWeDisplayPoints: Player whose points are displayed on the victory screen
withValueEventWichBeExecutedWhenThisEven: Dynamic value event that will be executed when this event is triggered. This allows you to create complex interactions and mechanics in your game, as you can trigger specific events with dynamic values based on the conditions of the game. For example, you could create a dynamic value event that calculates the number of points a player receives when they trigger a specific event, or that determines the next player to play based on the current game state.
eventHaveFromElementButNoFor: This event has a 'from' element but no 'for' element. This means that the event is triggered based on a specific source (e.g., a player, a card, or another game element), but it does not specify a target for the event's effects. This can be useful for creating events that are triggered by specific actions or conditions without directly affecting other elements in the game. However, it's important to ensure that the event's effects are still meaningful and contribute to the game's mechanics, even without a specified target.
